changes: Added `Event` struct (7 fields: ts, event, stage, provider, pid, exit_code, from, to)
Added `append_event()` function for JSONL WAL operations
Added 6 comprehensive unit tests covering nominal and edge cases

patterns_discovered.details: Uses `OpenOptions::new().append(true)` for atomic writes; enforces 512-byte size limit to stay within PIPE_BUF (4096) atomicity guarantee
Uses `#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]` to emit compact JSON without null fields
Uses `anyhow::Context` with `.with_context(|| ...)` for readable error messages with path context
Validates serialized event size before write; rejects oversized records with descriptive error
Tests cover: directory creation, multi-event appending, compact JSON format, optional field skipping, size rejection

gotchas_discovered.context: 512-byte max event size is enforced to guarantee atomic writes; exceeding PIPE_BUF (4096) breaks atomicity guarantee
gotchas_discovered.solution: Test `append_event_rejects_oversized_records()` catches this; runtime validation prevents silent data loss

gotchas_discovered.context: None fields omitted from JSON; test explicitly verifies `"provider"` absent when None
`logs/` directory created on first append_event() call; subsequent calls reuse existing directory
Used `serde_json::to_vec()` (compact) not `to_string_pretty()`; ensures predictable size for 512-byte check

approach_details.summary: Implemented JSONL write-ahead log append function with strict atomicity guarantees and comprehensive test coverage
approach_details.strategy: Storage decision from project memory: filesystem state machine + JSONL append-only event log. Event struct is flexible (optional fields) to support different event types. Size-limited records guarantee O_APPEND atomicity without locks.
approach_details.test_strategy: 6 unit tests covering: creation, multi-append, JSON format, optional fields, size rejection
approach_details.integration_status: Ready for use in dispatcher and merge-gate; complements existing `read_meta()` and `write_meta()` functions

recommendations.action: Add `read_events()` or `parse_events_jsonl()` function to consume audit log
recommendations.rationale: append_event() is one half of the WAL pattern; consumers (dashboard, lane_metrics, etc.) need to read and replay events

recommendations.action: Document Event schema in docs/ or add schema comment in Event struct
recommendations.rationale: External tools (vibekanban provider, dispatch.nu) may parse events.jsonl; schema must be discoverable

recommendations.action: Add event archival/rotation strategy for long-lived card directories
recommendations.rationale: Unbounded append will cause logs/ to grow indefinitely; consider moving old events to .tar.gz after card lands
Callers must manually construct Event with timestamp; reduce boilerplate in dispatcher/merge-gate

recommendations.action: Add telemetry aggregation function (event counts by type, timing analysis)
recommendations.rationale: lane_metrics.nu could consume events.jsonl for latency/throughput dashboards instead of parsing log files
